name: system-admin-mcp
description: System administration MCP with privilege separation for Windows systems
author: Your Organization

prompts.name: get_file_owner_prompt
prompts.description: Get the owner of a file or directory
prompts.template: Who owns the file or directory at {path}?

prompts.name: list_volumes_prompt
prompts.description: List all available volumes and their usage
prompts.template: Show me all available disk volumes and their usage information

prompts.name: get_disk_usage_prompt
prompts.description: Get disk usage for a specific path
prompts.template: How much disk space is used and available at {path}?

prompts.name: get_process_info_prompt
prompts.description: Get information about a running process
prompts.template: Show me information about process with ID {pid}

max_retries.description: Maximum number of retries for failed operations

documentation.overview: The System Admin MCP provides privileged system administration capabilities with proper privilege separation. It consists of a user-level bridge and an elevated Windows service.
documentation.installation: Install the MSI package to set up the elevated service, then install the DXT package for the bridge component.
documentation.usage: Interact with the MCP through the standard MCP interface. The bridge will automatically communicate with the elevated service when needed.
security_notes: The elevated service runs with SYSTEM privileges. Ensure proper access controls are in place.
All operations are logged for audit purposes.
The service only accepts local connections by default.
